top_signals.title: Oil Deepens Slide as Saudi Exports Reach 90% of Pre-War Levels
top_signals.summary: Oil deepened its slide below pre-war levels as Saudi Arabia ramped up crude exports toward normal rates, extending the recovery in flows from the Persian Gulf and heightening the prospect of a supply surplus.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Jefferson, Global Economic Developments and the U.S. Economy
top_signals.summary: Speech At the 2026 Bank of Japan-Institute for Monetary and Economic Studies Conference, Tokyo, Japan
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Live markets: bitcoin rises above $61,000 as U.S. jobs data for June disappoints
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Rivian raises 2026 delivery outlook after strong demand in the second quarter
top_signals.summary: Rivian is increasing its delivery outlook to between 65,000 and 70,000 EVs, up from 62,000 to 67,000 units.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Jeff Bezos' family office backed five AI startups in June
top_signals.summary: Bezos Expeditions is now the most active family office investor thus far this year, per data from Fintrx.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Minutes of the Board's discount rate meeting on April 20 and 29, 2026
top_signals.summary: Minutes of the Board's discount rate meeting on April 20 and 29, 2026

top_signals.title: US Hiring Slows Sharply, Curbing Recent Momentum in Job Growth
top_signals.summary: US hiring slowed sharply in June even as the unemployment rate fell, curbing some of the budding momentum in job growth this year.

top_signals.title: U.S. economy added 57,000 jobs in June, less than expected; unemployment rate at 4.2%
top_signals.summary: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to rise by 115,000 in June while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%, according to the Dow Jones consensus.
